title: John Milius’s CV
text:
Computer Science Student, Data Engineering Minor, BYU-Idaho

Jwmilius@gmail.com | 714-318-8646 | LinkedIn | GitHub



Expected Spring 2028 Brigham Young University – Idaho

Bachelor of Computer Science, Minor in Data Engineering
GPA: 4.0




Jul. 2025 - Jan. 2026 Elevate Fulfillment, Idaho Falls, ID — Warehouse Associate

Pick, pack, and fulfill high-volume customer orders accurately while meeting daily productivity targets in a fast-paced warehouse environment.
Operate forklifts and material handling equipment safely to transport, organize, and stage inventory, ensuring compliance with warehouse safety protocols.
Utilize ShipWise and internal fulfillment software to process shipments, update order status, and coordinate with cross-functional team members to achieve broader operational objectives.

Dec. 2024 - Apr. 2025 Matson CPA, Los Alamitos, CA — Office Assistant

Organized and safeguarded confidential client tax documents, ensuring accurate filing practices and compliance with privacy standards.
Responded to client inquiries and phone calls with professional communication, directing requests and supporting office workflow efficiency.

Jun. 2022 - Jun. 2024 The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, Denver, CO — Volunteer

Gained experience in cold-call techniques and interpersonal skills with a 70+ hour work week.
Provided effective, interactive training to large groups in over a dozen meetings.
Appointed as group leader; provided motivation and management of more than 16 representatives.




Python, MySQL Warehouse Managing System

Engineered automated shipment receipt processing system using Python XML parsing to handle incoming inventory, generating unique unit IDs and tracking shipment history through MySQL database with foreign key relationships.
Designed and implemented normalized relational database schema with 10+ tables managing products, locations, inventory units, and shipment tracking using MySQL Workbench; created migration scripts and seed data for warehouse configuration.

React/JSX, Node.js, MySQL Social Networking Web App

Collaborated with a team of four to develop a mobile-responsive web application enabling students to discover and connect with nearby peers and campus events.
Integrated front-end components with the backend database by implementing API endpoints in Node.js, dynamically loading user-specific data into React profile pages.
Ensured seamless data flow between the client and server by writing and testing SQL queries to support real-time profile updates.




Python, JavaScript, MySQL, C#, Git/GitHub, VS Code, JetBrains, Computer Hardware, Data Structures, Web Development, Databases, React/JSX, Node.js, Unit Tests




Certifications: Red Cross CPR/AED Certified (Feb. 2025)
Work Eligibility: Eligible to work for any U.S. Employer; open to relocation for internship opportunities.
